reasoning: The 12-month CD rate (NDR12MCD) has been dropping consistently in 2025 (from 1.83% in Dec 2024 to 1.62% in Jun 2025), reflecting banks lowering deposit rates in response to current economic conditions or anticipated rate cuts. Interest rates typically follow random walks rather than mean-reverting quickly to historical linear estimates, so the FRED model's P(exceed) prediction of 0.71 is misleading here. Given the steady and strong downward momentum over the last several months, the probability of an increase by July 8 is very low. Over longer horizons (September, December), the probabilities edge closer to 50% because the macroeconomic environment or Fed policy could shift, introducing more uncertainty, but the near-term momentum strongly suggests further rate declines or stability.
